units.title: The family
units.topic: Family members and relationships

words.description: A woman who has children; the female parent
words.example: My mother works as a teacher.

words.description: A man who has children; the male parent
words.example: My father is a doctor.

words.description: A male child in relation to his parents
words.example: They have one son and two daughters.

words.description: A female child in relation to her parents
words.example: Karen is Anne and Ivan's daughter.

words.description: A boy or man with the same parents as you
words.example: Jack is Karen's brother.

words.description: A girl or woman with the same parents as you
words.example: Karen is Jack's sister.

words.description: A man who is married; the male partner in a marriage
words.example: Ivan is Anne's husband.

words.description: A woman who is married; the female partner in a marriage
words.example: Anne is Ivan's wife.

words.description: Your mother and father together
words.example: Anne and Ivan are Karen and Jack's parents.

words.description: The sons and daughters of a person
words.example: They have two children: Karen and Jack.

words.description: The father of your mother or father
words.example: Henry is Karen and Jack's grandfather.

words.description: The mother of your mother or father
words.example: Diana is their grandmother.

words.description: The parents of your mother or father
words.example: Henry and Diana are the grandparents.

words.description: The son of your son or daughter
words.example: Jack is Henry and Diana's grandson.

words.description: The daughter of your son or daughter
words.example: Karen is Henry and Diana's granddaughter.

words.description: The brother of your mother or father
words.example: George is Karen and Jack's uncle.

words.description: The sister of your mother or father
words.example: Amelia and Meena are Karen and Jack's aunts.

words.description: The son of your brother or sister
words.example: Jack is their nephew.

words.description: The daughter of your brother or sister
words.example: Karen is their niece.

words.description: The child of your aunt or uncle
words.example: Kavita and Ravi are Karen and Jack's cousins.

words.description: Members of your family; people related to you
words.example: We have relatives living in many different cities.

words.description: A child who has no brothers or sisters
words.example: Have you got any brothers and sisters? No, I am an only child.

units.title: Birth, marriage and death
units.topic: Life events: being born, getting married, and dying

words.description: To give birth to a child
words.example: Anna had a baby yesterday.

words.description: To come into the world at birth
words.example: He was born at 1.15 yesterday morning.

words.description: To have a particular weight
words.example: The baby weighed 3 kilograms.

words.description: The anniversary of the day you were born
words.example: His grandfather's birthday is June 16th.

words.description: To give someone a particular name
words.example: They are going to call him John, after his grandfather.

words.description: Not married; without a partner
words.example: If you do not have a partner, you are single.

words.description: Having a husband or wife
words.example: If you have a husband or wife, you are married.

words.description: To become husband and wife in a ceremony
words.example: Harry and Sarah got married in 2001.

words.description: A ceremony where two people get married
words.example: The wedding was a beautiful ceremony.

words.description: A woman on her wedding day
words.example: The bride wore a beautiful white dress.

words.description: A man on his wedding day
words.example: The groom waited nervously at the altar.

words.description: A holiday taken by a newly married couple after the wedding
words.example: They went on their honeymoon to Italy.

words.description: Having lost your husband or wife through death
words.example: If your husband or wife dies, you are widowed.

words.description: Living apart from your husband or wife but not yet divorced
words.example: If your marriage breaks up, you are separated or divorced.

words.description: No longer legally married
words.example: The marriage has legally ended — they are divorced.

words.description: Not well; sick
words.example: Then Harry became ill.

words.description: To stop living
words.example: He died last year.

words.description: To die as a result of a particular cause or illness
words.example: He died of a heart attack.

words.description: No longer alive. Note: say 'Harry is dead' NOT 'Harry is died'
words.example: Harry is dead.

words.description: A ceremony held after someone dies before burial or cremation
words.example: The funeral was attended by many friends and family.

words.description: A sudden serious medical condition where the heart stops working normally
words.example: My father had a heart attack last year.

units.title: Parts of the body
units.topic: Names of the main parts of the human body

words.description: The thin threads growing from the skin on your head. Used as singular: 'My hair is long' NOT 'My hairs are long'
words.example: My hair is very long — I must cut it soon.

words.description: One of the two organs in your face that you use to see
words.example: She has blue eyes.

words.description: The part of your face above your mouth used for smelling and breathing
words.example: He broke his nose playing football.

words.description: The hard white objects in your mouth. Singular: tooth, plural: teeth
words.example: Brush your teeth twice a day.

words.description: The opening in your face used for eating and speaking
words.example: Open your mouth and say 'ah'.

words.description: One of the two organs on the sides of your head that you use to hear
words.example: She wore earrings on her ears.

words.description: Either of the two soft edges around the opening of the mouth
words.example: He has thin lips.

words.description: The part of the body connecting the head to the shoulders
words.example: She wore a scarf around her neck.

words.description: The joint connecting the arm to the upper body
words.example: He carried the bag over his shoulder.

words.description: The hard thin covering at the end of your fingers and toes
words.example: She painted her nails red.

words.description: The short thick finger at the side of your hand, separate from the other four
words.example: He gave a thumbs-up to say everything was OK.

words.description: One of the four long thin parts at the end of your hand
words.example: She wore a ring on her finger.

words.description: The part at the end of your arm including fingers and thumb
words.example: Wash your hands before eating.

words.description: The long part of your body from shoulder to hand
words.example: She broke her arm in the accident.

words.description: One of the two long parts of your body used for walking
words.example: She hurt her leg playing sport.

words.description: The joint in the middle of the leg that allows it to bend
words.example: She hurt her knee while running.

words.description: The part at the bottom of your leg that you stand on. Singular: foot, plural: feet
words.example: My feet are sore after walking all day.

words.description: One of the five parts at the end of your foot
words.example: He broke his toe.

words.description: The front of the body between the neck and the stomach; contains the heart and lungs
words.example: The doctor listened to his chest with a stethoscope.

words.description: The organ in your body that digests food; also the area below the chest
words.example: I have a pain in my stomach.

words.description: The rear part of your body from shoulders to hips
words.example: He has a bad back from sitting all day.

words.description: Either of the two parts of the body between the chest and the hip
words.example: I have a pain in my side.

words.description: The narrow part of your body between your chest and hips
words.example: She has a very slim waist.

words.description: The area on each side of the body between the waist and the top of the leg
words.example: She fell and broke her hip.

words.description: The outer covering of the human body
words.example: We have skin covering our bodies.

words.description: The organ inside your chest that pumps blood around your body
words.example: The heart beats about 70 times a minute.

words.description: The organ inside your head that controls thought, memory and feeling
words.example: The human brain is very complex.

words.description: The red liquid that flows through your body
words.example: He lost a lot of blood in the accident.

units.title: Clothes
units.topic: Types of clothing, accessories, and verbs for wearing clothes

words.description: A long warm piece of clothing worn over other clothes outdoors
words.example: Take a coat — it will be cold tonight.

words.description: A short coat with sleeves, worn over other clothes
words.example: She wore a green jacket to the meeting.

words.description: A piece of clothing for the upper body with a collar and buttons
words.example: He always wears a white shirt to work.

words.description: A simple shirt with short sleeves and no collar or buttons
words.example: He wore a blue T-shirt and jeans.

words.description: A warm knitted piece of clothing for the upper body
words.example: She put on a sweater because it was cold.

words.description: A one-piece garment for women covering the body from shoulders downward
words.example: She bought a beautiful red dress for the wedding.

words.description: A piece of clothing worn by women that hangs from the waist down
words.example: She wore a long skirt to the party.

words.description: A piece of clothing covering the lower body with separate legs; 'pants' in American English. Always plural
words.example: He wore black trousers to the interview.

words.description: Trousers made from denim. Always plural
words.example: She always wears jeans at the weekend.

words.description: Short trousers ending above or at the knee. Always plural
words.example: He wore shorts on the beach.

words.description: A tight covering for the legs and lower body, worn by women. Always plural
words.example: She wore black tights with her skirt.

words.description: A set of matching jacket and trousers worn for formal occasions
words.example: He wore a dark blue suit to the job interview.

words.description: A covering worn on the head, usually with a brim
words.example: She wore a big red hat to the party.

words.description: A long piece of cloth worn around the neck or head for warmth or style
words.example: He wrapped a scarf around his neck on the cold day.

words.description: Coverings for the hands with separate parts for each finger. Always plural
words.example: Don't forget your gloves — it's freezing outside.

words.description: Coverings for the feet. Always plural
words.example: She bought new shoes for the party.

words.description: Comfortable sports shoes; called 'sneakers' in American English
words.example: I wear trainers when I go to the gym.

words.description: Strong shoes that cover the foot and ankle or higher up the leg
words.example: She wears boots in winter to keep her feet warm.

words.description: Soft coverings for your feet, worn inside shoes. Always plural
words.example: He always wears white socks.

words.description: A strip of leather or cloth worn around the waist to hold up trousers
words.example: His trousers were too big, so he wore a belt.

words.description: A long piece of cloth worn around the neck, usually by men for formal occasions
words.example: He wore a tie to the business meeting.

words.description: A small clock worn on the wrist
words.example: He looked at his watch and realised he was late.

words.description: A circular piece of jewellery worn on a finger
words.example: She wore a gold ring on her left hand.

words.description: Lenses in a frame worn over the eyes to help you see better. Always plural
words.example: She wears glasses for reading.

words.description: Dark glasses worn to protect your eyes from bright sunlight. Always plural
words.example: Don't forget your sunglasses at the beach.

words.description: A bag carried by women for personal belongings
words.example: She's carrying a suitcase and a handbag.

words.description: A device with a circular canopy on a stick used as protection from rain
words.example: You carry a bag and an umbrella.

words.description: To have clothing or accessories on your body. Note: you WEAR clothes but CARRY bags
words.example: Naomi is wearing a long blue coat.

words.description: To place clothing on your body. Note: 'put clothes ON' not 'put off'
words.example: In the morning you get dressed or put your clothes on.

words.description: To remove clothing from your body. Note: 'take clothes OFF' not 'take off clothes'
words.example: At night you get undressed or take your clothes off.

units.title: Describing people
units.topic: Words for describing height, weight, hair, face and age

words.description: Having greater than average height. Note: say 'People are tall' NOT 'People are high'
words.example: Bettina Schwenke is a very tall woman.

words.description: Having less than average height
words.example: Tom Jakes is quite short.

words.description: Neither tall nor short; average height
words.example: If you aren't tall or short, you are of medium height.

words.description: Thin in an attractive, healthy way. More polite than 'thin'
words.example: Agata Sanchez is really slim.

words.description: Having little fat on the body; not fat
words.example: I was very thin when I was in hospital.

words.description: Heavier than is considered healthy
words.example: The doctor said I am overweight.

words.description: Having too much flesh on the body. Note: it is not polite to say this to someone
words.example: Their cat is very fat. It needs to go on a diet.

words.description: Having a darker skin colour
words.example: Suri has dark skin and dark hair.

words.description: Having a light, pale skin colour
words.example: Polly has fair skin and blonde hair.

words.description: Light yellow coloured hair. Note: say 'blonde/dark hair' NOT 'blonde/dark hairs'
words.example: Polly has blonde hair and fair skin.

words.description: Black or very dark brown hair
words.example: Suri has dark skin and dark hair.

words.description: Hair growing on a man's chin and cheeks
words.example: Ben has a beard and long hair.

words.description: Hair grown on the upper lip
words.example: Luca has a moustache and short hair.

words.description: Very attractive; usually used for women. 'My mother is a very beautiful woman'
words.example: My mother is a very beautiful woman.

words.description: Attractive in appearance; usually used for men
words.example: My dad's a very good-looking man.

words.description: Attractive; usually used for girls or women
words.example: My sister is very pretty.

words.description: Not attractive; the opposite of beautiful or good-looking
words.example: Bob's an ugly man.

words.description: Neither attractive nor unattractive; ordinary looking
words.example: I'm not ugly or beautiful, I'm just average-looking!

words.description: Having lived for a long time; not young
words.example: My grandmother is 97. She's very old.

words.description: Having lived for a short time; not old
words.example: My sister is 14. She's young.

words.description: Between about 40 and 60 years old
words.example: My father is 56. He's middle-aged.

words.description: Old; a polite word for old people
words.example: This hospital is for elderly people.

units.title: Health and illness
units.topic: Words for health conditions, common illnesses, and wellbeing

words.description: In a very healthy and good state
words.example: I'm very well, thanks.

words.description: Feeling good and healthy
words.example: I'm fine, thanks.

words.description: To feel slightly ill or not fully healthy
words.example: I don't feel very well. I must go home and rest.

words.description: To feel sick with an illness
words.example: I feel ill. Can you get a doctor, please?

words.description: Feeling like you want to vomit; also used to mean ill in American English
words.example: That fish was bad. I think I'm going to be sick!

words.description: A continuous pain in your head
words.example: Have you got an aspirin? I've got a headache.

words.description: A pain in one or more of your teeth
words.example: I've got toothache. I need to go to the dentist.

words.description: A common illness causing a runny nose and sore throat
words.example: I'm going to bed with a hot drink. I've got a cold.

words.description: A doctor who treats your teeth and mouth
words.example: I've got toothache. I need to go to the dentist.

words.description: An allergy to pollen causing sneezing and watery eyes
words.example: I get hay fever every summer from flowers and grass.

words.description: To suddenly blow air out of your nose and mouth uncontrollably
words.example: I sneeze all day because of hay fever.

words.description: A condition that makes it difficult to breathe
words.example: My little brother has asthma; sometimes he can't breathe.

words.description: A small flying insect that bites and can spread diseases
words.example: In some countries, mosquitoes can give people malaria.

words.description: A serious disease spread by mosquitoes in tropical countries
words.example: The drinking water was bad, and many children had cholera.

words.description: A serious disease caused by cells growing in an uncontrolled way
words.example: Every year cancer kills many people who smoke.

words.description: A building where sick or injured people receive medical treatment
words.example: My father went to hospital when he had a heart attack.

words.description: A sudden serious condition where the heart stops working normally. Note: 'had a heart attack' NOT 'got a heart attack'
words.example: My father had a heart attack.

words.description: To eat food that is good for your health
words.example: Do you have a healthy diet? Yes, I eat lots of fruit and vegetables.

words.description: Physical activity done to keep the body fit and healthy
words.example: Do you exercise? Yes, I like swimming, jogging and cycling.

words.description: Beneficial for your health
words.example: Swimming, jogging and cycling are really good for you.

words.description: Feeling worried and anxious because of problems or too much work
words.example: I feel stressed! I need to relax more.

words.description: To become calm and free from stress
words.example: You need to relax more and don't panic about work.

units.title: Feelings
units.topic: Words for emotions, preferences and desires

words.description: To feel very strong liking or affection; the strongest positive feeling (++)
words.example: I love my family and my best friend.

words.description: To find something pleasant or enjoyable (+)
words.example: I like my job.

words.description: To not enjoy or find something pleasant (-)
words.example: I don't like horror films.

words.description: To have a very strong feeling of dislike (--)
words.example: I hate spiders.

words.description: Feeling pleasure and satisfaction; not sad. Note: 'happy about' or 'happy for' NOT 'happy for'
words.example: I am very happy about your news.

words.description: Feeling unhappy or sorrowful
words.example: He was sad when his dog died.

words.description: Feeling or showing strong displeasure
words.example: She was very angry when she heard the news.

words.description: Feeling unhappy and worried because something bad has happened
words.example: He was upset when his friend didn't call.

words.description: Feeling a low temperature; needing warmth
words.example: I'm cold. Can you turn the heating on?

words.description: Feeling very warm; feeling too much heat
words.example: I'm hot. Can you open a window?

words.description: Feeling the need to drink water or other liquids
words.example: I'm thirsty. Can I have some water, please?

words.description: Feeling the need to eat food
words.example: I'm hungry. When is dinner?

words.description: In good health; feeling healthy
words.example: He's very well, thanks.

words.description: Not feeling healthy; sick
words.example: She looks ill. She should see a doctor.

words.description: Feeling a need to rest or sleep; lacking energy
words.example: I'm very tired. I need to sleep.

words.description: Feeling astonishment at something unexpected
words.example: She was surprised when she saw him at the party.

words.description: To like one thing more than another
words.example: I prefer coffee to tea. (= I like coffee more than I like tea.)

words.description: To want something to happen and believe it might. Note: 'I hope to do' or 'I hope that' NOT 'I hope doing'
words.example: I hope to do well in my exam.

words.description: To feel a desire for something. Note: 'I want you to help me' NOT 'I want that you help me'
words.example: I want a new car. I want to buy a new car.

units.title: Conversations 1: Greetings and wishes
units.topic: Everyday greetings, farewells, and special occasion expressions

words.description: A greeting used from waking up until midday (about 12pm)
words.example: Good morning! How are you today?

words.description: A greeting used from midday until about 6pm
words.example: Good afternoon, can I help you?

words.description: A greeting used in the evening, from about 6pm onwards
words.example: Good evening, welcome to the restaurant.

words.description: What you say when you leave someone or end a conversation
words.example: Goodbye! See you tomorrow.

words.description: An informal farewell used when you expect to see someone again soon
words.example: Goodbye! See you soon!

words.description: What you say when someone goes to bed. Note: only say goodnight when LEAVING, not when arriving somewhere
words.example: Goodnight! Sleep well.

words.description: Used when making a polite request
words.example: Can I have a coffee, please?

words.description: What you say when someone does something nice for you
words.example: Thank you for your help.

words.description: An informal British way to say thank you
words.example: Cheers! Thanks for buying the coffee.

words.description: Used to politely get someone's attention or to pass by someone
words.example: Excuse me, can you tell me the way to the station?

words.description: Used to apologise when you have done something wrong
words.example: Sorry! I didn't mean to bump into you.

words.description: What you say when someone sneezes
words.example: Achoo! — Bless you! — Thank you.

words.description: What you say to someone on their birthday. Note: say 'Happy Birthday' NOT 'Congratulations'
words.example: Happy Birthday! How old are you today?

words.description: What you say at Christmas time
words.example: Happy Christmas! Are you spending it with family?

words.description: What you say on or around New Year's Day
words.example: Happy New Year! I hope this year is a good one.

words.description: What you say when someone is about to do something difficult like an exam
words.example: Good luck with your exam tomorrow!

words.description: What you say when someone has achieved something special or done something well in an exam or had a baby
words.example: Congratulations! I heard you passed your exam.

words.description: What you say to praise someone who has done something well
words.example: Well done! That was an excellent presentation.

units.title: Conversations 2: Useful words and expressions
units.topic: Common words and expressions used in everyday conversation

words.description: In reality; used to say what is really true. Note: in English means 'in reality' NOT 'now' — it is a false friend in some languages
words.example: People say bad things about her, but she's actually very nice.

words.description: Very; used to emphasise something
words.example: The book is really good.

words.description: In addition to or different from what has been said
words.example: Do you want to buy anything else? Or go somewhere else?

words.description: Approximately; about a particular number or time
words.example: I'll meet you at around 6 o'clock.

words.description: Used to give a reason, or to return to an earlier subject in conversation
words.example: I'll drive you home anyway. — Anyway, as I said, I woke up very late today.

words.description: Used to make a suggestion to a group. Note: 'Why don't we go...' NOT 'Why don't we going...'
words.example: Why don't we go to the cinema this evening?

words.description: Used to suggest doing something together. Note: 'Let's go...' NOT 'Let's going...'
words.example: Let's go and see a film and then have a meal.

words.description: Used to make a suggestion or ask someone's opinion. Note: 'How about going...' NOT 'How about go...'
words.example: How about the new Angelina Jolie film?

words.description: It is the same to me; I have no preference
words.example: I don't mind. It's up to you.

words.description: You can decide; it is your choice
words.example: I don't mind. It's up to you.

words.uzbek: Voy! Ne balo! (hayrat yoki xafa bo'lganda)
words.description: Used when you are surprised or disappointed about something
words.example: Oh dear! Was your boss angry?

words.description: It is not important; don't worry about it
words.example: I forgot to bring your book. Oh, it doesn't matter. I don't need it.

words.description: Used when you are disappointed or sorry about something
words.example: I'm sorry, but I can't come to your party. What a pity!

words.description: Used to tell someone to be quick
words.example: Hurry up! The taxi's here.

words.description: Used to warn someone about danger
words.example: Look out! There's a car coming.

words.description: Used when you agree strongly with a suggestion or statement
words.example: Absolutely! What about getting her a scarf?

words.uzbek: Roziman, qo'shilaman. Note: 'I agree' NOT 'I am agree'
words.description: Used to say you have the same opinion. Note: NOT 'I am agree'
words.example: I agree. Let's go shopping this afternoon.

words.description: Used to praise someone who has done something well
words.example: I passed my exam! Well done!

units.title: Food and drink
units.topic: Common food items, fast food, fruit, vegetables, and drinks

words.description: A basic food made from flour and water, baked in an oven. Note: 'some bread' NOT 'a bread'
words.example: Would you like some bread?

words.description: Small white or brown grains cooked and eaten as a staple food
words.example: I love sushi because I love rice.

words.description: An Italian food made from flour and water in various shapes such as spaghetti
words.example: Pasta is good for you, but don't eat too much!

words.description: A white mineral used to flavour food
words.example: I always put salt on my chips, but not pepper.

words.description: A hot spicy powder used to flavour food
words.example: I always put salt on my chips, but not pepper.

words.description: The flesh of animals eaten as food, such as beef, chicken or lamb
words.example: My sister never eats meat or fish. She's vegetarian.

words.description: A sea or river animal used as food
words.example: My sister never eats meat or fish.

words.description: A person who does not eat meat or fish
words.example: She's vegetarian, so she doesn't eat meat or fish.

words.description: A sweet white or brown substance used to sweeten food and drinks
words.example: Do you take sugar in tea or coffee?

words.description: A hot drink made from dried leaves of the tea plant
words.example: Do you take sugar in your tea?

words.description: A hot drink made from roasted coffee beans
words.example: I have a cup of coffee every morning.

words.description: A cooked patty of minced beef served in a bread roll
words.example: I eat hamburgers when I don't have much time.

words.description: A cooked sausage served in a long bread roll
words.example: I eat hot dogs and pizzas when I don't have much time.

words.description: An Italian dish of flat bread covered with tomato, cheese and toppings
words.example: I eat hamburgers, hot dogs and pizzas when I don't have much time.

words.description: A traditional British meal of fried fish with fried potato chips
words.example: Fish and chips is popular in Britain, Australia and New Zealand.

words.description: The sweet product of a plant. Note: 'fruit' is uncountable — 'I love fruit' NOT 'I love fruits'
words.example: Fruit is also good for you.

words.description: Plants or parts of plants eaten as food such as carrots and onions
words.example: Vegetables are good for you.

words.description: Long orange root vegetables
words.example: Carrots, beans, potatoes and peas are all vegetables.

words.description: Small seeds inside a pod, eaten as a vegetable
words.example: Carrots, beans, potatoes and peas are all vegetables.

words.description: Round starchy vegetables that grow underground
words.example: She made soup with potatoes, tomatoes and onions.

words.description: Red round fruit usually used as a vegetable in cooking
words.example: She put cheese and tomato in her sandwich.

words.description: Small round green seeds eaten as a vegetable
words.example: Carrots, beans, potatoes and peas are all vegetables.

words.description: Round vegetables with a strong taste, used in cooking
words.example: The soup has onions, garlic and mushrooms in it.

words.description: A plant with a strong smell and taste, used in cooking
words.example: The soup has onions, garlic and mushrooms in it.

words.description: A type of fungus eaten as a vegetable
words.example: The soup has onions, garlic and mushrooms in it.

words.description: A round fruit, red, yellow or green, with a sweet or sour taste
words.example: An apple a day keeps the doctor away.

words.description: A round orange citrus fruit with a sweet juice
words.example: I drink orange juice every morning.

words.description: A long curved yellow fruit
words.example: She had a banana for a snack.

words.description: A sweet juicy fruit that is narrow at the top and wider at the bottom
words.example: She ate a pear after lunch.

words.description: Small round green or purple fruits that grow in bunches
words.example: She bought a bunch of grapes.

words.description: Small red sweet fruits
words.example: I love strawberries and cream.

words.description: A large tropical fruit with a rough brown skin and sweet yellow flesh
words.example: She bought a pineapple from the market.

words.description: A white liquid produced by cows and other animals, used as a drink
words.example: Do you want milk in your tea?

words.description: A drink made from the liquid of crushed or squeezed fruit
words.example: I drink orange juice every morning.

words.description: An alcoholic drink made from grain
words.example: He ordered a beer at the pub.

words.description: An alcoholic drink made from grapes
words.example: She drank a glass of wine with dinner.

words.description: Water from a natural spring, often sold in bottles
words.example: I'll have a mineral water, please.

units.title: In the kitchen
units.topic: Kitchen appliances, utensils, and things used for cooking and eating

words.description: A machine that keeps food cold; short for refrigerator
words.example: Put the milk in the fridge.

words.description: A machine or compartment that freezes food at very low temperatures
words.example: The ice cream is in the freezer.

words.description: An oven that heats food very quickly using microwaves
words.example: Heat the soup in the microwave.

words.description: A device for cooking food with a hob and usually an oven
words.example: She cooked the pasta on the cooker.

words.description: A machine that washes dishes automatically
words.example: Put the plates in the dishwasher.

words.description: A machine that washes clothes
words.example: Can you put this in the washing machine?

words.description: A basin with taps fixed to a wall for washing hands and dishes
words.example: Wash the cups in the sink.

words.description: A device that controls the flow of water from a pipe
words.example: Turn the tap on to get water.

words.description: A piece of furniture or space in a wall with a door, used for storing things
words.example: The cups are in the cupboard.

words.description: A flat board fixed to a wall or inside a cupboard for storing things
words.example: The books are on the shelf above the desk.

words.description: A container for putting rubbish in
words.example: Put the empty packet in the bin.

words.description: The flat surface in a kitchen where food is prepared
words.example: Leave the bread on the worktop.

words.description: A deep metal pot with a handle used for cooking on the hob
words.example: Boil the water in a saucepan.

words.description: A flat pan with a long handle used for frying food
words.example: Fry the eggs in a frying pan.

words.description: A pot with a spout used for making and serving tea
words.example: She made a pot of tea in the teapot.

words.description: A cloth used for drying dishes and hands in the kitchen
words.example: Use the tea towel to dry the plates.

words.description: A machine used to brew coffee
words.example: She turns on the coffee maker every morning.

words.description: A roll of thick absorbent paper used for cleaning in the kitchen
words.example: She tore a piece of kitchen roll to clean up the spill.

words.description: A piece of fabric used for cleaning or covering surfaces
words.example: Wipe the table with a cloth.

words.description: Liquid soap used for washing dishes by hand
words.example: Put some washing-up liquid on the sponge.

words.description: A flat round dish used for eating or serving food
words.example: Put your dinner on the plate.

words.description: A round deep dish used for eating food like soup or cereal
words.example: She ate a bowl of soup.

words.description: A small container with a handle used for drinking hot drinks
words.example: She drank a cup of tea.

words.description: A large cup with straight sides and a handle
words.example: He drinks coffee from a big mug.

words.description: A container made of glass used for cold drinks
words.example: Can I have a glass of water?

words.description: A tool with a sharp blade used for cutting food
words.example: Use a knife to cut the bread.

words.description: A tool with prongs used for picking up and eating food
words.example: Use a fork to eat your pasta.

words.description: A tool with a round bowl used for eating liquid food or stirring
words.example: Use a spoon for the soup.

words.description: Two thin sticks used for eating food in Asian countries
words.example: Can you eat with chopsticks?

words.description: To prepare food by heating it
words.example: You can cook some rice.

words.description: To remove water from something by rubbing or leaving in air
words.example: You can dry the plates with the tea towel.

units.title: In the bedroom and bathroom
units.topic: Furniture and objects in bedrooms and bathrooms, and daily routines

words.description: A piece of furniture for sleeping on
words.example: Emma goes to bed at 11 o'clock.

words.description: A small lamp placed on the table next to your bed
words.example: She reads by the bedside lamp.

words.description: A clock that makes a sound to wake you up at a set time
words.example: She wakes up when her alarm clock rings.

words.description: Loose comfortable clothing worn for sleeping. Always plural
words.example: She gets undressed and puts on her pyjamas.

words.description: A table with a mirror used for doing your hair and make-up
words.example: She sits at the dressing table to do her hair.

words.description: A piece of furniture with several drawers for storing clothes
words.example: His T-shirts are in the chest of drawers.

words.description: A glass surface that reflects your image so you can see yourself
words.example: She looked in the mirror before leaving.

words.description: A large piece of furniture with doors for hanging and storing clothes
words.example: He hung his suits in the wardrobe.

words.description: A small table placed next to the bed
words.example: She put her book on the bedside table.

words.description: A flat piece of plastic or metal with thin teeth for arranging hair
words.example: He runs a comb through his hair each morning.

words.description: A brush with a handle used for smoothing and styling hair
words.example: She brushes her hair with a hairbrush.

words.description: A device that sprays water over you for washing your body
words.example: She has a shower every morning.

words.description: A substance used with water for washing your body or hands
words.example: Wash your hands with soap.

words.description: A liquid soap used for washing hair
words.example: She uses shampoo to wash her hair.

words.description: A liquid soap used in the shower for washing the body
words.example: He uses shower gel when he has a shower.

words.description: A substance put on a toothbrush to clean teeth
words.example: She puts toothpaste on her toothbrush.

words.description: A small brush used for cleaning teeth
words.example: She cleans her teeth with a toothbrush.

words.description: A sharp instrument used for shaving
words.example: He uses a razor to shave in the morning.

words.description: A bowl with a seat used for getting rid of body waste
words.example: The toilet is in the bathroom.

words.description: A bowl fixed to the wall in a bathroom for washing your hands and face
words.example: Wash your face in the basin.

words.description: A piece of absorbent cloth used for drying yourself after washing
words.example: She dried herself with a towel after the shower.

words.description: To stop sleeping; to become conscious
words.example: She wakes up when her alarm clock rings.

words.description: To get out of bed and start the day
words.example: She gets up and goes downstairs.

words.description: To wash yourself under a shower
words.example: She has a shower and cleans her teeth.

words.description: To put on your clothes
words.example: She has a shower and gets dressed.

words.description: To take off your clothes
words.example: She gets undressed and gets into bed.

words.description: To get into bed in order to sleep
words.example: Emma goes to bed at 11 o'clock.

words.description: To start sleeping
words.example: She turns off the light and falls asleep.

words.description: To move to a higher floor of a building
words.example: She goes upstairs to her bedroom.

words.description: To move to a lower floor of a building
words.example: She goes downstairs to the kitchen for breakfast.

units.title: In the living room
units.topic: Furniture and objects in the living room, and related verbs

words.description: A shelf or set of shelves for storing books. Singular: bookshelf, plural: bookshelves
words.example: The books are on the bookshelf in the living room.

words.description: A long comfortable seat with a back and arms, for two or more people
words.example: We sit on the sofa when we watch TV.

words.description: A large comfortable chair with supports for your arms
words.example: Grandpa always sits in the armchair.

words.description: A separate seat for one person, with a back
words.example: Please pull up a chair and sit down.

words.description: A piece of furniture with a flat top and legs
words.example: The remote control is on the table.

words.description: A small low table in front of the sofa in a living room
words.example: She put her coffee cup on the coffee table.

words.description: A device with a screen that shows moving pictures and sound; television
words.example: Every evening I watch television.

words.description: A device used to operate a TV or other electronics from a distance
words.example: Can you pass me the remote control? I want to turn on the TV.

words.description: A device that produces light, usually electric
words.example: She turned on the lamp to read.

words.description: A switch on the wall used to turn lights on or off
words.example: The light switch is on the wall by the door.

words.description: A painting or photograph displayed on the wall
words.example: There are beautiful pictures on the walls.

words.description: An opening in a wall covered with glass to let in light and air
words.example: Open the window — it's hot in here.

words.description: Pieces of fabric hung at windows to block light and give privacy. Always plural
words.example: She opened the curtains to let in the morning sunlight.

words.description: A thick fabric covering for the whole floor of a room
words.example: The carpet in the living room is red.

words.description: A small carpet that covers only part of a floor
words.example: There is a rug in front of the sofa.

words.description: A device used for making and receiving calls
words.example: The phone is on the table.

words.description: A device fixed in the wall where you plug in electrical equipment
words.example: Plug the lamp into the socket.

words.description: High quality sound equipment for playing music
words.example: He listens to music on his hi-fi.

words.description: An electric lamp fixed to the ceiling or wall of a room
words.example: Can you turn the light on? It's getting dark.

words.description: To view programmes on TV. Note: 'watch TV' NOT 'see TV'
words.example: Every evening I watch television.

words.description: To hear programmes broadcast on the radio. Note: 'listen to the radio' NOT 'hear the radio'
words.example: Sometimes I listen to the radio or listen to music.

words.description: To pull the curtains across the window to block light
words.example: It's getting dark. Can you close the curtains, please?

words.description: To turn on a light using a switch
words.example: OK. And I'll switch the light on.

words.description: To start operating the television
words.example: Thanks. Now can you turn the radio off and pass me the remote control? I want to turn on the TV.

words.description: Large moveable objects in a room such as tables, chairs and sofas. Note: 'furniture IS white' NOT 'furniture ARE white'
words.example: The furniture in my room is white.

units.title: Jobs
units.topic: Types of jobs and places where people work

words.description: A person trained in medicine who treats sick or injured people
words.example: The doctor told me to rest for a week.

words.description: A person whose job is to educate students in a school or other setting
words.example: My English teacher is very patient.

words.description: A person trained to care for sick or injured people in hospitals
words.example: The nurse checked my blood pressure every morning.

words.description: A person who repairs machines, especially cars and engines
words.example: The mechanic fixed my car in two hours.

words.description: A person who works in an office doing typing, filing and administrative tasks
words.example: She works as a secretary in a law firm.

words.description: A person who serves and helps customers in a shop
words.example: The shop assistant helped me find the right size.

words.description: A person who cuts and styles hair, usually in a salon
words.example: I want to work in a beauty salon as a hairdresser.

words.description: A person who designs and builds machines, structures or systems
words.example: She works as a software engineer at a tech company.

words.description: A person who owns or manages a farm and grows food or raises animals
words.example: The farmer lives on a farm and works in the fields.

words.description: A member of the police force who maintains law and order
words.example: The police officer stopped the car.

words.description: A person whose job is to check that cars are parked legally
words.example: The traffic warden gave me a parking ticket.

words.description: A person who works in a library helping people find books and information
words.example: The librarian helped me find the book I needed.

words.description: A person who works in a bank dealing with customers' accounts and transactions
words.example: The bank clerk processed my transaction quickly.

words.description: A man who serves food and drinks in a restaurant
words.example: I'm a waiter. I work in a restaurant.

words.description: A person who drives a taxi and transports passengers for payment
words.example: I'm a taxi driver. I like my job.

words.description: A regular paid position of employment
words.example: What's your job? I'm a waiter.

words.description: The place or activity where you earn money
words.example: Where do you work? I work in an office.

words.description: A building where goods are manufactured or assembled
words.example: My dad works in a factory which makes car parts.

words.description: A place where goods are sold to customers
words.example: I worked in a shop at the weekends when I was a student.

words.description: A room or building where people work at desks doing administrative tasks
words.example: I work in an office. Sometimes it's boring.

words.description: A building where sick or injured people receive medical treatment
words.example: I'd like to work in a children's hospital.

words.description: A place where people pay to eat meals
words.example: I'm a waiter. I work in a restaurant.

words.description: A place where people go to have their hair cut and beauty treatments
words.example: I want to work in a beauty salon as a hairdresser.

units.title: At school and university
units.topic: School subjects, classroom objects, and education vocabulary

words.description: The English language studied as a school subject
words.example: She's very good at English.

words.description: The study of numbers, shapes and patterns (mathematics)
words.example: A maths teacher teaches maths. Her students study maths.

words.description: The study of events that happened in the past
words.example: We study the First World War in history.

words.description: The study of the Earth's surface, countries, climate and natural features
words.example: We learn about mountains and rivers in geography.

words.description: The scientific study of living things — plants, animals and people
words.example: We study plants and animals in biology.

words.description: The subject where you draw, paint and create visual works
words.example: She loves art and wants to be a painter.

words.description: The scientific study of substances and how they react with each other
words.example: We did experiments in chemistry class.

words.description: The science that studies matter, energy, forces and motion
words.example: Physics is difficult but very interesting.

words.description: The subject where you learn to play instruments or sing
words.example: She plays violin in music class.

words.description: School lessons about sport, exercise and physical fitness
words.example: We play football in PE.

words.description: The study of computers and information technology
words.example: We learn to use computers in ICT.

words.description: The study of foreign languages such as French, Spanish or German
words.example: She studies French and Spanish as modern languages.

words.description: A large flat surface on a classroom wall where the teacher writes
words.example: The teacher wrote the new words on the board.

words.description: A board on a wall where notices and information are pinned up
words.example: She pinned the announcement on the noticeboard.

words.description: A thin wooden stick with a dark centre used for writing that can be erased
words.example: Draw the diagram with a pencil so you can erase mistakes.

words.description: A writing instrument that uses ink
words.example: Can I borrow your pen? I need to sign this.

words.description: A small book with blank or lined pages used for writing notes
words.example: Write the vocabulary in your notebook.

words.description: A book used for studying a particular subject at school or college
words.example: Open your textbook to page 12.

words.description: A piece of rubber used to remove pencil marks; called 'eraser' in American English
words.example: Can I borrow your rubber? I made a mistake.

words.description: A device for sharpening the point of a pencil
words.example: My pencil is blunt. Do you have a pencil sharpener?

words.description: A flat straight instrument used for measuring and drawing straight lines
words.example: Use a ruler to draw a straight line.

words.description: A table at which students sit to study or work
words.example: Sit at your desk and open your books.

words.description: A white shiny board you write on with special pens that can be erased
words.example: The teacher drew a diagram on the whiteboard.

words.description: An electronic machine for storing and processing information
words.example: Students learn to use computers at school.

words.description: A short pin with a flat head used for fixing paper to boards
words.example: She used a drawing pin to put the poster on the noticeboard.

words.description: A single sheet of paper for writing on
words.example: Write your answers on a piece of paper.

words.description: To attend school as a student
words.example: Children go to school and students go to university.

words.description: To complete school work at home. Note: 'do homework' NOT 'make homework'
words.example: After school, students do homework.

words.description: To succeed in an examination
words.example: You hope to pass your exams.

words.description: To be unsuccessful in an examination
words.example: You don't want to fail your exams.

words.description: To complete a university course and receive a formal qualification
words.example: If you pass your final exams at university, you get a degree.

units.title: Communications
units.topic: Letters, email, internet, telephones, and mobile devices

words.description: A written message sent by post to someone
words.example: She wrote a letter to her friend in Paris.

words.description: The details of where someone lives, used for sending letters
words.example: What's your address? I want to send you a letter.

words.description: A small piece of paper stuck on a letter to show that postage has been paid
words.example: Don't forget to put a stamp on the envelope.

words.description: A flat paper container for a letter
words.example: Put the letter in the envelope before you post it.

words.description: The day, month and year written on a letter or document
words.example: Don't forget to write the date on your letter.

words.description: A box fixed to a post or wall where letters are posted and collected
words.example: She dropped the letter in the letter box.

words.description: To send a letter or package by mail
words.example: Don't forget to post the letters.

words.description: An electronic machine used for processing information and accessing the internet
words.example: Olivia gets a lot of emails from New York.

words.description: The flat display part of a computer or phone
words.example: The screen of his computer is very large.

words.description: The set of keys used to type on a computer
words.example: She types very fast on the keyboard.

words.description: A small device moved by hand to control a cursor on a computer screen
words.example: Use the mouse to click on the icon.

words.description: A portable computer that can be used on your lap or anywhere
words.example: She works on her laptop at home.

words.description: A small portable device for storing computer data
words.example: Save the file on a memory stick.

words.description: An electronic message sent over the internet
words.example: Olivia gets a lot of emails from New York.

words.description: A unique address used to send and receive emails
words.example: What's your email address? It's Moll at C-U-P dot com.

words.description: Connected to or available through the internet
words.example: You have to be careful what information you give people online.

words.description: A portable telephone that can be used anywhere
words.example: What's your mobile number?

words.description: A device for communicating by voice over a distance
words.example: The telephone was ringing when I arrived home.

words.description: A small public enclosure containing a pay telephone
words.example: There's a phone box on the corner of the street.

words.description: A portable electronic device such as a smartphone or tablet
words.example: I always take my mobile device with me.

words.description: An act of speaking to someone by telephone
words.example: Juan makes a lot of phone calls every day.

words.description: The number you dial to make a phone call to someone
words.example: What's your phone number? 066530718.

words.description: A recorded message left when someone does not answer their phone
words.example: He's not answering his phone. I'll leave a voicemail.

words.description: A short written message sent by mobile phone; to send such a message
words.example: I'll text him. / Send him a text message.

words.description: To communicate with someone, especially on the phone
words.example: Hello. It's Nick here. Can I speak to Ahmed, please?

words.description: To phone someone again or to return their phone call
words.example: Could you just tell him I called? I'll call back later.

units.title: Your phone
units.topic: Parts of a smartphone and vocabulary for using it

words.description: The part of a smartphone used for taking photos and videos
words.example: The camera on this phone takes excellent photos.

words.description: The touchscreen display area of a phone
words.example: The screen of his phone is very large.

words.description: Short for applications; programs downloaded onto a smartphone
words.example: There are many useful apps on my phone.

words.description: The button used to turn the phone on or off
words.example: Press the power button to turn the phone on.

words.description: A protective cover for a smartphone
words.example: She bought a pink phone case for her new phone.

words.description: A device used to recharge the battery of a phone
words.example: My phone is dead. Where's the charger?

words.description: The component that stores electrical power for the phone
words.example: The battery is flat. I need to charge my phone.

words.description: A small card inside a phone that stores your phone number and connects you to a network
words.example: You need to put a SIM card in your new phone.

words.description: A photo you take of yourself, usually with a smartphone held at arm's length
words.example: Ramesh takes a lot of selfies and posts them online.

words.description: A recorded message left on a phone when no one answers
words.example: Lisa didn't answer, so I left a voicemail.

words.description: To send a short written message using a mobile phone
words.example: Can you text me the address of the restaurant?

words.description: A piece of written or spoken communication sent to someone
words.example: I've just got a message from Andy.

words.description: To copy an application from the internet to your phone
words.example: You can download an app with a dictionary to help you.

words.description: To phone someone; to make a telephone call
words.example: While she was on holiday, Kelly called her family every day.

words.description: A phone screen that is secured and requires a code or gesture to open
words.example: It's locked. What's the PIN?

words.description: Personal Identification Number; a secret number used to unlock a phone or use a bank card
words.example: You don't need a PIN to unlock it. Just swipe the screen.

words.description: To move your finger smoothly across a touchscreen
words.example: Just swipe the screen left to right to unlock it.

words.description: To store data, photos or files on a device
words.example: I can't save any new photos. My phone memory is full.

words.description: To permanently remove data, photos or messages from a device
words.example: Your phone memory is full. You need to delete some photos.

words.description: The storage space on a phone for saving photos, apps and data
words.example: Your phone memory is full.

units.title: Holidays
units.topic: Types of holidays, transport, and things needed for travelling

words.description: A period of time when you travel somewhere or rest away from home and work
words.example: We had a lovely holiday in Egypt in 2014.

words.description: Currently away from work or study for leisure
words.example: I'm not working next week. I'm on holiday.

words.description: To leave home to travel somewhere for leisure
words.example: Are you going on holiday this summer?

words.uzbek: to'liq xizmat ko'rsatish bilan ta'til (parvoz va mehmonxona birgа)
words.description: A holiday where flights and accommodation are booked and paid for together
words.example: We're going on a package holiday to Hong Kong.

words.description: Spending time outdoors sleeping in a tent
words.example: I want to go camping this year — I'll sleep in a tent.

words.description: A holiday spent walking in the countryside or mountains
words.example: I'm going on a walking holiday in the Alps.

words.description: A holiday where you travel by a comfortable bus visiting many places
words.example: A coach tour is an easy way to go on holiday.

words.description: A holiday taken during the winter months, often for skiing
words.example: We're going to have a winter holiday this year.

words.description: Travelling using an aircraft as transport
words.example: Are you flying to France? Are you going by plane?

words.description: Travelling using a train as transport
words.example: No, we're going by train.

words.description: Travelling using a car as transport
words.example: We drove there by car.

words.description: Travelling on a boat that makes regular crossings of water
words.example: No, we're going by ferry.

words.description: Travelling using a long-distance comfortable bus
words.example: We went by coach — it was cheaper than the train.

words.description: An official document proving your identity used for international travel
words.example: You need a passport to travel to another country.

words.description: An official stamp or document in your passport allowing you to enter a country
words.example: A visa is a stamp that you need in your passport to go to some countries.

words.description: Documents allowing you to travel on transport or enter a place
words.example: Don't forget your tickets.

words.description: The money used in a particular country
words.example: Bring some currency — the money of the country you are going to.

words.description: The bags and suitcases you take with you when travelling. Note: uncountable — NOT 'luggages'
words.example: Your luggage, e.g. a suitcase or a rucksack.

words.description: A large rectangular bag with a handle used for carrying clothes when travelling
words.example: She packed her suitcase the night before the trip.

words.description: A large bag carried on the back, used for walking or travelling
words.example: He carried his camping equipment in a rucksack.

words.description: To eat the traditional food of the country or place you are visiting
words.example: We want to try the local food and enjoy the nightlife.

words.description: A wish for someone to enjoy themselves on their trip or holiday
words.example: Have a great time! And send me a postcard!

words.description: A card with a picture on the front, sent as a greeting from a holiday destination
words.example: Send me a postcard from your holiday!

units.title: Shops and shopping
units.topic: Types of shops, signs in shops, and shopping expressions

words.description: A shop that sells meat; also the name of the person who sells or prepares meat
words.example: I'm going to the butcher's to get a chicken.

words.description: A building where you can post letters, buy stamps and collect parcels
words.example: I need to go to the post office to send a parcel.

words.description: A large self-service shop selling food and household goods
words.example: I'll get everything we need from the supermarket.

words.description: A shop that sells books
words.example: I bought this novel at the bookshop on the high street.

words.description: A shop that sells bread and cakes; also the person who makes bread
words.example: She bought fresh bread from the baker's.

words.description: A large shop divided into departments selling many types of goods — clothes, furniture, food, toys etc
words.example: A department store is a large shop which sells a lot of different things.

words.description: A shop that sells medicines, cosmetics and beauty products; called 'pharmacy' in American English
words.example: I need to go to the chemist's to get my medicine.

words.description: A shop that sells newspapers, magazines, sweets and cigarettes
words.example: I'm going to the newsagent's to get a paper.

words.description: A shop that sells souvenirs and items suitable for giving as presents
words.example: She bought a souvenir at the gift shop.

words.description: The lowest floor of a building, at street level
words.example: The food section is on the ground floor.

words.description: The floor above the ground floor in British English
words.example: Women's Wear is on the first floor.

words.description: A floor below the ground level of a building
words.example: The electricals and food sections are in the basement.

words.description: Signs on shop doors showing whether the shop is open or closed for business
words.example: The sign on the door said CLOSED so I couldn't get in.

words.description: Signs on doors telling you whether to push or pull to open them
words.example: The sign on the door said PUSH, so I pushed it open.

words.description: To put on clothing to see if it fits or suits you before buying it
words.example: Can I try this shirt on? Have you got a bigger size?

words.description: To have a particular price
words.example: How much does it cost? It costs £22.

words.description: To give money in exchange for goods or services
words.example: Can I pay by cheque?

words.description: A plastic card used to pay for goods now and pay the bill later
words.example: No, credit card, debit card or cash, please.

words.description: Physical money in the form of notes and coins
words.example: Can I pay by cash? — Yes, of course.

words.description: A piece of paper money
words.example: I only have a £50 note. I don't have any change.

words.description: The money returned to you when you pay more than the price of something
words.example: I only have a £50 note. I don't have any change.

words.description: A piece of paper proving that you have paid for something
words.example: Here's your receipt. Would you like a bag?

units.title: Online shopping
units.topic: Vocabulary for shopping on the internet

words.description: To buy goods using the internet
words.example: Lisa likes to shop online because it's quick and easy.

words.description: Written opinions about a product or service from customers who have used or bought it
words.example: Reading online reviews can help you choose what to buy.

words.description: The virtual container where you place items when shopping online before paying
words.example: Click 'Add to basket' to buy the item.

words.description: The process of bringing goods to your home after ordering them online
words.example: If you are not at home to receive the delivery, we will take it to the post office.

words.description: To click the button to select an item you want to buy when shopping online
words.example: Click 'Add to basket' to select the item.

words.description: The final stage of an online purchase where you pay for your items
words.example: Go to checkout to complete your order.

words.description: A card for making payments online or in shops
words.example: Most people pay for their online shopping with credit cards.

words.description: A request to buy goods; a purchase made online or in a shop
words.example: Your order number is 5624890.

words.description: A unique reference number given to each online purchase
words.example: Please quote your order number in any enquiries.

words.description: The measurement of something, especially clothes; e.g. small, medium, large
words.example: What size is this T-shirt? Small, medium or large?

words.description: The amount of money something costs
words.example: The price of the T-shirt is £6.99.

words.description: The visual quality of something such as blue, red or green; used when choosing items online
words.example: Available in three colours: white, blue and red.

words.description: A service where goods are delivered to you the day after you order them
words.example: Next Day Delivery costs £5.99.

words.description: A service where goods are delivered to you at no extra cost
words.example: Free Delivery takes 3-5 days.

words.description: A code at the end of a postal address that helps sort mail; called 'zip code' in the US
words.example: A post code is a long number or mix of letters at the end of your address.

words.description: To remove a product from your online shopping basket
words.example: Click 'Delete this item' to remove it from your basket.

words.description: The address where you want your online order to be sent
words.example: Please fill in your delivery address carefully.
